chapter_title.ar: باب زَكَاةِ الْمَالِ الْغَائِبِ وَ الدَّيْنِ وَ الْوَدِيعَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – Zakāt of the hidden wealth, and the debts, and the deposits

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/40)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/40)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَنْسَى أَوْ يُعِينُ فَلَا يَزَالُ مَالُهُ دَيْناً كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ فِي زَكَاتِهِ قَالَ يُزَكِّيهِ وَ لَا يُزَكِّي مَا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الدَّيْنِ إِنَّمَا الزَّكَاةُ عَلَى صَاحِبِ الْمَالِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Ali Bin Al Numan, from Abu Al Sabbah Al Kinany,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the man who forgets or assists (the borrower) and his wealth does not cease to remain as outstanding debts. How should he deal with his Zakāt?’ Heasws said: ‘He should purify it (pay Zakāt), and he would not pay for what is upon him from the debts. But rather, the Zakāt is upon the owner of the wealth’.
en.sarwar: 12. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Ali ibn al-Nu‘man from ibn al- Sabbah al-Kinaniy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who does not collect what he has lent because of forgetfulness or the desire to help his borrowers so his assets continue to remain as loans.How must he deal with Zakat? He (the owner of the assets) not the borrower pays Zakat.’”
